subject: [PATCH v4 5/8] fuse: {io-uring} Allow reduced number of ring queues

reviews.author: Bernd Schubert
reviews.summary: reviewer noted that even if buffers are associated with a specific queue, the current patch still allows for dynamic registration of queues and suggested this should also be possible with pBuf
reviews.raw_body:
If the buffer is associated with queue, queues still can be registered
dynamically? I don't say that your should be forced to do dynamic
queues, it is just that the current reduced queue patches allow to have
that. And with pBuf I don't see why it shouldn't work either.

Thanks,
Bernd

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author initially misunderstood the patch as addressing dynamic queues instead of registered buffers, but after re-reading it, confirmed that it would be compatible with registered buffers and suggested a possible adaptation for dynamic queues.
reviews.raw_body:
I got the buffer pinning mixed up with dynamic queue ents, not dynamic
queues. I reread your patch and it would be compatible with the
registered buffers interface. Dyanmic queues would work with
group_cpus_evenly() as well I believe, we would just need to rebuild
the cpu-to-qid mapping for each new queue, but this seems ok since
it's a fixed registration cost.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-04-27.patch_summary: This patch allows FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) servers to use registered buffers/zero copy with a reduced number of ring queues, which is less than the total number of CPUs on the machine. This change addresses a request from server administrators who want to optimize memory allocation and usage. The approach taken is to relax the constraint on the number of ring queues required for zero-copy operations.
